title: Creamy Pesto Shrimp Pasta with Cherry Tomatoes
description: Blue Apron fan favorite — plump shrimp and burst cherry tomatoes tossed with linguine in a rich, creamy basil pesto sauce. An elegant pasta dinner that comes together in just 25 minutes.
instructions: Cook linguine in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Pat shrimp dry and season with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add shrimp in a single layer; cook 1–2 minutes per side until pink and just cooked through. Remove to a plate.
In the same pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add minced shallot and garlic; cook 2 minutes until softened.
Add white wine and cook 1 minute, scraping up any browned bits. Add cherry tomatoes; cook 3–4 minutes until they begin to burst.
Reduce heat to medium-low. Add heavy cream and pesto; stir to combine. Simmer 2 minutes.
Add drained pasta and toss to coat, adding pasta water a splash at a time to reach desired consistency.
Return shrimp to the pan and toss gently. Remove from heat and stir in parmesan and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Serve garnished with fresh basil leaves and extra parmesan.
tips: Don't overcook the shrimp — they only need 1–2 minutes per side. They'll continue cooking slightly in the hot sauce.
Reserve more pasta water than you think you need — it's the secret to a silky, restaurant-style sauce.
Store-bought pesto works great here; Costco's refrigerated pesto is excellent.
For a lighter version, substitute half-and-half or whole milk for the heavy cream.
